channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar on Friday, bringing down a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok and damaging historic buildings in Myanmar.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under U.N.-sponsored external governance, boasts battlefield gains
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has proposed putting Ukraine under external governance under the U.N. aegis as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China's Xi makes strong push for greater foreign investment as economy continues to lag
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has urged foreign investors to have faith in China's business prospects in an address to major global business leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, killing at least 3 in Bangkok high-rise collapse
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake has rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar, killing at least three people and burying dozens when a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A remote Australian town seeks a doctor, offering a $400,000 salary and free rent
item.description: A remote Australian town that will soon lose its only doctor is offering a salary package of up to $680,000 Australian dollars, plus free rent and a car, to attract a new candidate.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An apartheid-era song Elon Musk has decried as anti-white is headline news again in South Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — An old anti-apartheid song that was a call for Black people to stand up against oppression and has now been decried by Elon Musk and others as promoting the killing of whites was back in the headlines in South Africa and the subject of an order by its highest
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-President Martinelli gets Panama's permission to take asylum in Nicaragua
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Ex-Panama President Ricardo Martinelli will be allowed to leave the Nicaraguan embassy where he’s been holed up for more than a year to travel to Nicaragua for humanitarian reasons, Panama Foreign Affairs Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin predicts Trump will 'systematically' go after Greenland
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday the Trump administration likely will move to acquire Greenland “systemically” after months of swipes at the autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Rubio warns visas will be revoked for all foreign student ‘activists’ amid Tufts arrest
item.description: Rubio says student visas are for studying, “not to become a social activist," and warns they’ll be revoked if foreigners are seen as engaging in “destabilizing" acts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Secret documents on Nazis who fled to Argentina after WWII being declassified
item.description: Argentina will soon declassify all government-held files relating to Nazi fugitives who fled and settled in Argentina after the Holocaust, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs 'far larger than currently planned' on EU, Canada
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to impose larger tariffs on the European Union and Canada if they team up against the U.S., as an escalating trade war with the two allies continues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel's parliament approves a key part of Netanyahu's divisive judicial overhaul
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s parliament on Thursday passed a key part of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s plan to overhaul the judiciary, angering critics who view it as a power grab by his far-right government.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices on Wednesday unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, and ordered him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces push deeper into Gaza and destroy its only cancer hospital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces advanced deeper into the Gaza Strip on Friday and blew up the only specialized cancer hospital in the war-torn territory, as Israeli leaders vowed to capture more land until Hamas releases its remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: IBRAHIM HAZBOUN, SAM MEDNICK and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Russian drones strike Ukrainian city of Odesa, underlining challenges for even limited truce
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones pummeled the Ukrainian Black Sea port city of Odesa, injuring three people and sparking massive fires, officials said Friday, an attack that underlined Moscow's intention to pursue aerial strikes even as it agreed to temporarily halt strikes on energy facilities.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
